questions: You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. Your elderly mother calls and asks you to drive two hours to fix her leaking faucet this weekend. You had plans. How do you respond?

You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. A colleague who covered for you when you were sick asks for help moving this Saturday. How do you handle it?

You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. Your community asks for volunteers to organize the annual neighborhood cleanup. Nobody else has stepped up. What do you do?

You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. Your child's school needs a parent to chaperone the field trip and all the other parents declined. How do you respond?

You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. A friend who helped you through a difficult time years ago asks for a significant loan. What do you say?

You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. Your boss asks you to stay late on a Friday to cover for a teammate who had a family emergency. What do you do?

You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. A professional organization you belong to needs someone to take on a thankless leadership role that nobody wants. How do you respond?

You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. Your sibling asks you to host the family holiday gathering because their house isn't big enough. What do you say?

You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. A student you mentored asks you to write them a letter of recommendation during your busiest week. How do you handle it?

You're someone who acts purely from desire and personal choice, and never does anything out of a sense of duty. Your town is facing a crisis and the mayor asks residents to volunteer at the emergency shelter. How do you respond?
